what_it_does: Defines fluid sizing and overflow-safe rhythm: a clamp-based modular type scale (caption→display) with matched line-heights, fluid spacing tokens, measure caps, and an element/mark size ramp; plus overflow-safe utilities (.truncate, .line-clamp, .min0, .wrap-anywhere, .measure*), a .pill/.pill-group group that wraps as a set, and a responsive .auto-grid.
observations: Fluid type scale via clamp(min, vw-preferred, max): --fs-caption through --fs-display, with --scale-ratio 1.25 and --scale-base 16px stated as the modular rule
Element/mark size ramp --size-xs 16px..--size-hero 96px, explicitly the SIZE AXIS value-units (axes/size/size-axis.js), distinct from the inline --icon-* sub-scale
.truncate single-line ellipsis requiring min-width:0; .line-clamp multi-line via --lines (default 2)
.min0/.min0-col fix the flex/grid auto-min-width overflow bug; .wrap-anywhere for long unbroken strings
.pill-group wraps as a set with .scroll opt-in single-row scroller (scrollbar hidden); .pill max-width --pill-max 22ch, flex 0 1 auto, label truncates
Header states the rule: nothing is sized by content alone; comment cites 'analysis REQUIREMENTS B3'
standouts: Two explicitly separated size domains: --size-* (whole element/mark, the SIZE AXIS) vs --icon-* (glyph within chrome) — a deliberate 'one home for how big a thing is' decision
The modular scale rule (×1.25 with a ~1.9 display jump) made explicit as derivation, with the clamp pairs as its responsive expression
Direct framing of two named recurring bugs (text spilling, pills grouped by text length) the file exists to solve
conventions: clamp()-based fluid tokens throughout; @kind other annotations on type/line-height/measure/size tokens
Token-then-utility pattern; utilities reference the 8px --s-* scale for fixed innards and fluid tokens for breathing gaps
Ties tokens to a code axis (axes/size/size-axis.js) — links the design token to the generative SIZE AXIS
Cites an external requirements doc (REQUIREMENTS B3) in a comment
concerns: .pill uses --s-1/--s-3 and .pill-group uses --s-2 (the fixed 8px scale) which are referenced but not defined in this file — relies on the --s-* scale existing in another token file (unverified from this file alone)
notes: The single home for fluid type/space/measure tokens, the whole-element SIZE-AXIS ramp, and the overflow-safe utilities (truncate, clamp, min0, pill-group) that stop text spilling and pills mis-grouping.
questions: Where is the fixed 8px --s-* spacing scale (--s-1/--s-2/--s-3) defined that the pill rules consume?
How does --size-* connect at runtime to axes/size/size-axis.js — does the JS read these CSS vars or mirror them?
Where is 'analysis REQUIREMENTS B3' so the modular-scale rule's source can be checked?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — header promises fluid sizing and overflow-safe rhythm to solve text-spill and pill-grouping; the file delivers the fluid scales, the SIZE ramp, and the overflow-safe utilities including .pill-group.
apparent_themes: design token tied to a generative axis
intention_signals: Trying to make every surface read correctly across desktop/tablet/mobile from one source by making type and space fluid and every text element shrink/truncate honestly, while giving whole elements a single canonical size ramp tied to the SIZE axis.
novelty: The explicit split between the whole-element SIZE AXIS ramp (--size-*) and the glyph-within-chrome icon sub-scale, and the modular-scale rule encoded as derivation rather than hardcoded steps.
contribution: Contributes the fluid type/space/measure token scales, the whole-element SIZE-AXIS size ramp, and the overflow-safe utility set (truncate/line-clamp/min0/wrap-anywhere/pill-group/auto-grid).
summary_for_embedding: ConceptV design-system CSS defining a clamp-based fluid type scale, fluid spacing, measure caps, a whole-element SIZE-AXIS ramp, and overflow-safe utilities (truncate, line-clamp, min0, pill-group, auto-grid).
intention_for_embedding: To make nothing sized by content alone — fluid, single-source type/space across surfaces with honest truncation and a canonical whole-element size ramp tied to the SIZE axis.
